design_philosophy.core_loop: Place bombs to destroy blocks and monsters, flip switches to open gates, progress through maze-like areas to reach bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial awareness and timing: navigating mazes while managing bomb placement and avoiding explosions; multiplayer competition for territory and power-ups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Strategic maze combat and multiplayer party game dominance; single-player progression as protagonist fighting alien invaders
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Executes a 'ludicrously simple concept faultlessly' with perfect balance and superb control. Designed as a pure, mechanics-focused multiplayer experience where four-player chaos is the primary value proposition. Story mode exists but is secondary to competitive battle modes. Prioritizes functional, uncluttered design over narrative complexity.

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-world progression with boss encounters at end of each world
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up system (bomb count, firepower, speed, special abilities like remote control and bomb throwing) allows players to customize their capabilities. Story mode difficulty appears manageable; multiplayer difficulty scales with player count and skill matching.

progression.upgrade_system: Collectable power-ups embedded in destructible maze blocks: increased bomb count, increased firepower/range, movement speed boost, remote control bombs, throwing ability, extra hit protection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Destroy blocks to reveal power-ups; defeat bosses to advance to next world. Five worlds total with escalating difficulty.
replayability_hooks: 10 multiplayer battle arenas with customizable rules (battle royale or team matches)
G-Bomber mode: winner gets golden status and power-up bonus for next match (determined by spinning wheel)
4-player multitap support creating different competitive dynamics
Multiplayer balance and accessibility designed for repeated play sessions

world_building_techniques: Framing narrative: Five evil cyborgs (Five Dastardly Bombers) capture original Bomberman, player must rescue him and save Earth
Named boss characters with implied personalities: Magnet Bomber, Golem Bomber, Pretty Bomber, Brain Bomber, Plasma Bomber (leader)
Progressive escalation: starting in alien space station dungeon, progressing through four worlds to final confrontation
Each boss has two-phase encounter: first on foot, then retreats into giant machine

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as world capstones with mechanical escalation. Each boss presents two distinct phases (on-foot combat followed by machine-based battle), providing variety within single encounter. Thematic naming suggests different combat approaches.

faction_indicators: Five Dastardly Bombers as unified antagonist faction attempting universe conquest
G-Bomber mechanic creates temporary status hierarchy (winner gets golden state and advantages)
danger_signals: Alien invaders with machine technology represent existential threat to Earth
Capture and imprisonment narrative (Bomberman in space station dungeon)
Leader structure with Plasma Bomber as final boss suggests organized hierarchy
environmental_storytelling: Progression from prison dungeon through five distinct worlds culminating in final confrontation
Boss retreat into giant machines suggests technological escalation and adaptation
Maze-like areas filled with destructible blocks and switches create explorable hostile environments
